name: Beer Battered Soft Shell Crabs with Asian Dipping Sauce
recipeIngredient: 3 tablespoons Thai fish sauce (nam plo), available at specialty or Asian markets
2 thin slices of habanero, Scotch bonnet, or jalapeno chili, depending on desired degree of heat
4 small to medium soft shell crabs, cleaned and rinsed
salt and freshly ground black pepper
recipeInstructions:
1. In a bowl, make a well in the center of 1 cup all-purpose flour. Pour in 1 cup of beer and whisk until just combined.
2. Strain the batter through a sieve into a clean bowl and let it rest, covered, for 1 hour. Before using, whisk in additional beer if necessary to achieve desired consistency.
3. In a small bowl, whisk together lime juice, fish sauce, honey, water, garlic, scallion, and chili slices until dissolved.
4. Place cleaned crabs in a shallow dish and add enough milk to cover. Soak crabs for 10 to 15 minutes.
5. Heat peanut oil in a heavy deep pan to 350 degrees F. Season the crabs with salt and pepper.
6. Dust the crabs with flour, shaking off any excess. Coat and fry crabs two at a time. Dip each crab into the beer batter and allow extra batter to drip back into the bowl.
7. Carefully lower the crabs into the heated oil and fry until golden brown, turning once, about 2 minutes per side. Transfer fried crabs to paper towels to drain.
8. Repeat with remaining crabs. Arrange on individual serving plates. Garnish with scallion brushes, cilantro sprigs, and lime wedges. Serve immediately with dipping sauce on the side.
Yield: 4 servings
